name: Better Chatbot
what_it_does: An open-source AI chatbot platform that unifies multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, Ollama) in a single Next.js interface with MCP tool support, custom agents, visual workflow builder, and real-time voice.
use_cases: Self-hosted alternative to ChatGPT/Claude.ai with support for switching between multiple AI providers
Building and sharing custom AI agents with specialized instructions and MCP tool access
Creating reusable automation workflows via a visual builder and connecting them to MCP servers
not_for: Teams that need a production-grade, actively maintained SaaS platform — development is paused
Users who want a simple single-provider chat interface without the complexity of multi-model setup
Environments where security vulnerabilities cannot be self-managed (noted in README that community is patching issues)
best_when: You want a self-hosted, feature-rich chatbot that aggregates multiple LLMs and MCP tools in one place and you can manage your own deployment.
avoid_when: You need a stable, actively maintained platform with vendor SLA and security patches — development is currently paused.

auth.notes: Uses Better Auth library; supports OAuth providers for user authentication. API keys for LLM providers are configured by the user.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Costs are pass-through to LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.). Uses free tiers of Vercel, Neon, and Vercel Blob by default.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No specific documentation on error handling quality; community-maintained codebase.
known_agent_gotchas: Development is paused — security vulnerabilities may accumulate without active maintenance
MCP integration is as a client consuming other MCP servers, not a standalone MCP server
Multi-provider complexity means debugging issues can be hard to attribute to the platform vs LLM provider
